### Post by jazzman14 on 2008-05-02
Hi,

I have dual monitors set up and working well. the only thing i want to now do, is be able to drag applications between the two monitors. here is my xorg.conf:

```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

#

# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using

# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.

#

# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf

# For example:

#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg



Section "ServerLayout"

	Identifier	"Default Layout"

  Screen 0 "Screen0" 0 0

  Screen 1 "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"

	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"

	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"



EndSection



Section "Files"

EndSection



Section "Module"

	Load		"glx"

	Load		"GLcore"

	Load		"v4l"

EndSection



Section "InputDevice"

	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Driver		"kbd"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"

	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"

	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

EndSection



Section "InputDevice"

	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"

	Driver		"mouse"

	Option		"CorePointer"

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"

	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"

	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"

	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"

EndSection



Section "Device"

	Identifier	"Device0"

	Boardname	"ATI Radeon 8500"

	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"

	Driver		"ati"

	Vendorname	"ATI"

	Option		"MergedFB"	"off"

EndSection



Section "Device"  

	Identifier	"Device1"

	Boardname	"RIVA TNT2"

	Busid		"PCI:2:13:0"

	Driver		"nv"

	Vendorname	"RIVA"

EndSection



Section "Monitor"

	Identifier	"Monitor0"

	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"

	Modelname	"Monitor 1024x768"

	Horizsync	31.5-61.0

	Vertrefresh	50-75



  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync



	Gamma	1.0

EndSection



Section "Monitor" 

	Identifier	"Monitor1"

	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"

	Modelname	"Monitor 1024x768"

	Horizsync	31.5-61.0

	Vertrefresh	50-75



  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync



	Gamma	1.0

EndSection





Section "Screen"

	Identifier	"Screen0"

	Device		"Device0"

	Monitor		"Monitor0"

	Defaultdepth	24

	SubSection "Display"

		Depth	24

		Virtual	1024	768

		Modes		"1024x768@75"

	EndSubSection

EndSection







Section "Screen"

	Identifier	"Screen1"

	Device		"Device1"

	Monitor		"Monitor1"

	Defaultdepth	24

	SubSection "Display"

		Depth	24

		Virtual	1024	768

		Modes		"1024x768@75"	

	EndSubSection

EndSection



Section "ServerFlags"



EndSection
```

how can this be done? thanks!

### Post by Zorael on 2008-05-03
Try adding the following to the ServerLayout section. I think that's where it's supposed to be.
```
	Option	"Xinerama" "true"
```

---

### Post by jazzman14 on 2008-05-03
i added that line...and everything went completely downhill. i couldn't get any display...i restored back to the original xorg.conf.
any other ideas?

### Post by MatthewAPeters on 2008-05-03
Cross post: [HOW-TO: NVIDIA GeForce 6600GT with twinview in Hardy Heron]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=780777")

You may be able to pick something out of my xorg.conf - I just got twinview working.  Take the how-to with a grain of salt, as your main issue doesn't sound like a video card driver.
